global_cues_snapshot.dollar_index_dxy: ~101.20, firm - supported by US 10-year yields above 4.6% and rising odds of a Fed rate HIKE (not cut) in September (~53% priced in, up from 47% a day earlier) as oil-driven inflation risk resurfaces
global_cues_snapshot.crude_oil: Brent ~$91.2/bbl (highest since 10Jun26, +2.26% on the day), WTI ~$84-89/bbl - both up sharply on US-Iran war escalation (10th consecutive day of US airstrikes), Houthi threats to blockade Saudi shipping in the Red Sea, a tanker strike near the Strait of Hormuz, and a separate attack on the Caspian Pipeline Consortium terminal disrupting Kazakh exports
global_cues_snapshot.us_markets: S&P 500 ~7,509 (+0.89%, snapped a 3-day slide), Nasdaq +~1.3%, Dow +~0.7% - Tuesday's rebound driven by a semiconductor bounce (Micron +12%, Intel +8%, Marvell +6%) on strong Korean/Taiwanese chip export data, but tone turned cautious again entering Wednesday amid tech-earnings risk and oil/geopolitical concerns. 10-year Treasury yield >4.6%
global_cues_snapshot.europe_markets: STOXX 600 declined Wednesday as European tech shares (ASML, Infineon, STMicroelectronics, BE Semiconductor) fell in sympathy with prior Asian chip weakness, partially offset by energy-sector gains on higher oil prices
global_cues_snapshot.japan_markets: Nikkei 225 has been extremely volatile - swung from -2.12% (7Jul26) to +3%+ (21Jul26, ~66,232) as sentiment tracks global semiconductor swings; still up roughly +34% YTD in 2026 despite the volatility. A weaker yen boosts exporters' overseas earnings but raises import costs/inflation, a persistent two-sided driver of Nikkei moves
global_cues_snapshot.china_markets: Mixed/choppy - CSI 300 has swung between +3% and -1% sessions over the past two weeks; Hang Seng modestly positive recently, lifted at times by strength in domestic EV/consumer names (e.g. BYD)
global_cues_snapshot.asian_markets_broader: Kospi has been the most volatile major Asian index - fell ~5% in a single session in early July on a chip-sector rout (SK Hynix, Samsung both down sharply) before rebounding +3.56% by 21Jul26; still up roughly +85% YTD in 2026. Overall Asian tech sentiment remains the single biggest swing factor for regional and, by extension, Indian IT-adjacent sentiment
global_cues_snapshot.key_global_theme: Markets are caught between an AI/semiconductor-led risk-on impulse (periodic sharp rebounds) and a geopolitics/oil-led risk-off impulse (Iran war, Red Sea, Strait of Hormuz, Caspian pipeline disruptions) - both operating simultaneously and driving unusually high day-to-day volatility across global and Indian markets alike

data_quality_note: Index levels, DXY, oil prices and Fed-probability figures are sourced from live market reports as of 21-22Jul26 and are reasonably current at time of writing but will move intraday/daily - treat exact levels as indicative rather than real-time-precise. Scores (0-10 scale per the schema) are our own qualitative synthesis of the underlying news/data, not a published index.

dollar_index_impact.reason: Firm DXY (~101.2) alongside rising Fed rate-hike odds (53% priced for Sep) works against EM currency/equity inflows; this is precisely the dynamic behind the recent heavy FII selling in Indian financials

oil_price_impact.reason: Brent near $91 raises India's import bill and inflation risk, reducing RBI's room to cut rates further - directly negative for rate-sensitive banks/NBFCs

us_market_impact.reason: US market swings are currently semiconductor/tech-driven rather than financials-driven, so the read-through to Indian banks is muted either way

asian_market_impact.reason: Broader EM/Asian risk sentiment has been volatile on the chip-sector swings, and BFSI as India's most FII-owned, most liquid sector is the first target for any regional risk-off rotation

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Cautiously Improving if Oil/Fed Risk Eases
key_drivers: Resolution path of the Fed's Sep decision (hike vs hold) is the single biggest swing factor for FII flows into Indian financials
Any de-escalation in the Iran war / Strait of Hormuz situation would quickly ease oil-driven inflation fears and revive RBI rate-cut hopes
Government's LTCG tax waiver for FPIs on G-Secs is a direct, if narrow, attempt to stabilise capital flows relevant to the sector
Continued strong DII/SIP buying is expected to keep providing a floor regardless of the global backdrop

dollar_index_impact.reason: A firm dollar is a modest revenue-translation tailwind for IT services exporters, but is currently overshadowed by broader AI/semiconductor sentiment swings

oil_price_impact.reason: IT services have minimal direct oil-input exposure, though a sustained oil shock denting global (especially US corporate) growth could indirectly soften IT spending

us_market_impact.reason: Tuesday's US semiconductor rebound (Micron +12%, Intel +8%) is a positive signal for tech sentiment broadly, but follows repeated sharp AI-valuation-driven selloffs in the prior two weeks, keeping the read-through unstable

europe_market_impact.reason: European tech shares (ASML, Infineon, STMicro) fell on Wednesday in sympathy with Asian chip weakness, an incremental negative cross-current for global tech sentiment

japan_market_impact.reason: Nikkei's huge day-to-day swings (-2.1% to +3%+) are almost entirely semiconductor-sentiment-driven, directly mirroring the same AI-capex-anxiety cycle that swings Indian IT sentiment

asian_market_impact.reason: Kospi's rebound (+3.56% on 21Jul26) after an earlier ~5% chip-driven rout shows the same whipsaw pattern; strong Korean/Taiwanese export data was the direct trigger for Tuesday's global tech bounce that also lifted Nifty IT +4.3% for the week

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Volatile but Constructive if AI-Capex Holds
key_drivers: Global hyperscaler AI capex guidance (~$750bn 2026, guided toward $1tn+ in 2027) remains a structural positive for enterprise tech spending if it doesn't correct sharply
Repeated AI-valuation-driven selloffs (seen multiple times in Jul26 alone) mean sentiment could swing either way quickly - this is the key volatility risk for the sector over the next two quarters
US Q2/Q3 corporate earnings and tech capex guidance will be the most direct read-through for Indian IT services demand
A sustained oil shock denting global growth is a secondary, indirect risk to watch

current_global_impact.global_trend: Mixed - Bullish for Upstream, Bearish for OMCs

dollar_index_impact.reason: Oil is dollar-denominated; a firm DXY makes crude marginally more expensive for India as a rupee-based importer, compounding the price-driven cost pressure

oil_price_impact.reason: Brent near $91 (highest since 10Jun26) directly boosts upstream/E&P realizations (e.g. ONGC-type names) but squeezes refiner/OMC marketing margins and raises India's import bill and CAD risk

europe_market_impact.reason: European energy-sector gains (partially offsetting the STOXX 600's tech-led decline) mirror the same oil-price tailwind for the sector globally

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Elevated Volatility, Structurally Bullish for Upstream Near-Term
key_drivers: Strait of Hormuz and Red Sea shipping-route risk (Houthi threats) are direct, ongoing threats to global oil supply and freight costs
Any Iran-US ceasefire/de-escalation would quickly reverse the current oil-price premium, a key downside risk to monitor for upstream names
Reliance Industries' Q1FY27 results and continued capex direction are the most important single stock-level catalyst for the sector
OMC margin pressure from high crude is a persistent headwind unless government fuel-pricing policy offers relief

dollar_index_impact.reason: Firm dollar raises imported-component costs for auto manufacturers reliant on global supply chains (semiconductors, specialty materials)

oil_price_impact.reason: Elevated crude directly raises fuel costs for consumers and input/logistics costs for manufacturers, a clear near-term demand headwind

china_market_impact.reason: China EV competitiveness (e.g. BYD's continued sales strength) remains a structural competitive pressure on global and Indian auto/EV players

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Cautious - Oil Price the Key Swing Factor
key_drivers: Any easing in crude oil prices (contingent on Iran-war de-escalation) would be the single biggest positive catalyst for auto demand and margins
Festive-season (Oct-Dec) demand and monsoon-driven rural income recovery remain the main domestic offsetting positives
Global semiconductor supply/pricing volatility remains a secondary watch item for auto-component costs

dollar_index_impact.reason: A firm dollar is a direct revenue-translation tailwind for pharma companies with large US export/generics businesses

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude raises input costs for active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) manufacturing and logistics, a modest but real cost headwind

us_market_impact.reason: US market moves are currently tech/semiconductor-driven rather than healthcare-driven, limiting direct read-through

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Stable to Mildly Positive, Overhung by Wegovy-Competition Narrative
key_drivers: Novo Nordisk's India regulatory approval for Wegovy (obesity drug) remains a sector-specific overhang on domestic pharma/generics competitiveness sentiment
US FDA approval/inspection newsflow and US generic-pricing trends remain the most direct global driver for Indian pharma exporters
A firm dollar continues to support export economics even as sector sentiment stays cautious near-term

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude raises packaging, logistics and input costs (crude derivatives) across the FMCG value chain, and higher fuel/energy prices for consumers can compress discretionary spend

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Stable, Watching Input Costs and Rural Demand
key_drivers: Crude/energy price trajectory is the key global-cues factor for input costs and consumer purchasing power
Monsoon performance and rural income trends are the dominant domestic driver, largely independent of global market swings
Festive-season demand into Q4 is the key seasonal catalyst to watch

current_global_impact.global_trend: Mixed - China Demand and Dollar Sensitive

dollar_index_impact.reason: Metals are dollar-denominated commodities; a firm DXY is typically a headwind for metal prices and mining-company realizations

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude raises energy/input costs for metals production and smelting

china_market_impact.reason: China's choppy equity performance (CSI 300 swinging between +3% and -1% sessions) reflects broader uncertainty about Chinese demand, the single biggest external swing factor for metals

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Constructive on Domestic Capex, Global Demand Uncertain
key_drivers: Chinese demand/stimulus signals remain the dominant global swing factor for metal prices
Domestic infrastructure capex execution continues to provide an offsetting structural tailwind regardless of global price direction
A firmer-for-longer dollar (on Fed hike risk) would be a modest headwind to global metal price realizations

dollar_index_impact.reason: Sector is predominantly domestic-demand-driven, limiting direct currency sensitivity, though imported-component costs for some capital goods makers rise modestly with a firm dollar

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude raises input/logistics costs for construction and equipment manufacturing

us_market_impact.reason: Global hyperscaler AI/data-centre capex strength (the same theme driving CG Power's and ABB's order wins) is directly linked to robust US tech-infrastructure investment

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Positive - Domestic Capex Plus Global Data-Centre Demand
key_drivers: Continued global hyperscaler data-centre capex (power, automation, cooling equipment orders) is a genuine incremental export opportunity for Indian capital goods makers, largely insulated from near-term FX/oil volatility
Domestic government infrastructure capex execution remains the dominant driver
Oil-price-driven input cost inflation is a modest, manageable headwind rather than a major risk

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude/gas prices raise costs for gas-based power generation but make domestic coal/renewable generation relatively more cost-competitive

us_market_impact.reason: Global data-centre power-demand growth (explicitly flagged by ABB India management as a key order driver) directly benefits Indian power/electrification equipment names

key_drivers: Data-centre and AI-infrastructure-linked power demand is a structural, largely global-macro-independent tailwind
Grid-modernization and renewable capacity addition programs continue regardless of near-term FX/oil swings

dollar_index_impact.reason: Telecom equipment makers with import-linked components face modestly higher costs from a firm dollar

europe_market_impact.reason: Tejas Networks' NEC 5G MIMO partnership and export relationships give the sector some Asia/Europe demand linkage, though not a dominant global-market driver currently

key_drivers: Domestic ARPU growth and 5G/fibre rollout remain the dominant sector drivers, largely independent of the current global oil/dollar/tech-sentiment cross-currents
Continued export order wins (e.g. NEC partnership) are a modest incremental positive

dollar_index_impact.reason: Firm dollar and rising Fed rate-hike odds work against the same FII-flow and rate-cut dynamics that support real estate financing costs

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude raises construction material and logistics costs

key_drivers: Data-centre and commercial real estate demand (linked to global hyperscaler capex) is an emerging structural positive largely insulated from near-term FX/oil swings
Rate-cut timing (contingent on the Fed and RBI paths) remains the key macro swing factor for housing finance costs and demand

dollar_index_impact.reason: Many consumer durables (electronics components, compressors, chips) are import-linked, so a firm dollar raises input costs

oil_price_impact.reason: Higher crude raises logistics/input costs and can pressure discretionary consumer spending

us_market_impact.reason: Semiconductor price/supply volatility (evident in the sharp recent swings in chip stocks) is a direct cost input for electronics-heavy consumer durables

china_market_impact.reason: China remains a key source of components/inputs for electronics-heavy consumer durables manufacturing

expected_impact_next_2_quarters.overall_outlook: Stable, Watching Input Costs and Festive Demand
key_drivers: Festive-season demand (Oct-Dec) is the dominant seasonal driver
Semiconductor and component price volatility remains a global input-cost risk to monitor
A stabilising rupee (if oil/Fed risk eases) would help ease imported-component cost pressure
